Your response should be clear, concise, and directly address both agreement/disagreement with prior conclusions and explicitly state the selected answer.

I agree with the previous conclusion that cavity varnish primarily acts to reduce acid penetration into dentinal tubules. While it may have secondary benefits like slight thermal insulation due to its physical barrier properties or fluoride-mediated effects on tooth structure, these do not constitute its main therapeutic action. The core mechanism—sealing dentinal tubules—is consistently documented in dental literature.
